title: 🚀 Deployment Frequency [Edu] [v2]

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🚀 Deployment Frequency

**DORA Pillar: Throughput**

## What it Measures
Deployment Frequency measures how often your organisation successfully deploys code to production. It is one of the two DORA *throughput* metrics and reflects the pace at which your team delivers value to users.

## Why it Matters
Higher deployment frequency indicates a mature, well-automated CI/CD pipeline and a team that delivers small, incremental changes. Elite-performing teams deploy on demand (multiple times per day), while low performers deploy less than once per month.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Frequency | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | Multiple deploys/day (>7/wk) | On-demand delivery, small batch sizes |
| 🟡 High | 1–7 deploys/week | Regular cadence, good automation |
| 🟠 Medium | 1–4 deploys/month (<1/wk) | Larger batches, some manual steps |
| 🔴 Low | <1 deploy/month (<0.25/wk) | Infrequent releases, high risk per deploy |

## How to Take Action
- **If low**: Break work into smaller PRs, automate deployment pipelines, reduce manual approval gates
- **If declining**: Investigate CI/CD bottlenecks, review branch protection policies, check for flaky tests blocking deployments
- **If improving**: Document what's working, share practices across teams

panels.description: Deployment Frequency — DORA throughput pillar. Performance tiers and action guidance.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / GREATEST(1, EXTRACT(DAY FROM ($__timeTo()::timestamptz - $__timeFrom()::timestamptz)) / 7.0) AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
AND ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Deployment Frequency
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments API. Only GitHub-native deployments counted. 'Success' state is set by the deployer, not verified by GitHub.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Deployments per Week
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments API. Bucketed by date_trunc('week', created_at). Weeks with zero deployments appear as chart gaps.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  d.environment AS "Environment",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.title: Deployments by Environment
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments API. Environment = free-text field — 'prod' and 'production' count as separate environments.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ds.state = 'success' THEN d.deployment_id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0), 1
) AS "Success Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Deployment Success Rate
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployment Statuses API. 'Success' state is set by the API caller — not independently verified by GitHub.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Failed Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Failed Deployments Timeline
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployment Statuses (state='failure'). Does not capture state='error'. Retried deploys appear in both timelines.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  d.sha,
  d.environment,
  ds.state AS status,
  d.creator_login,
  d.created_at
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50

panels.title: Recent Deployments
panels.description: Source: GitHub Deployments + Statuses. creator_login = API caller (often a bot), not code author. LEFT JOIN — status may be NULL.

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Deployment Frequency: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description: ⚠️ Cohort by deployer's seat status. Fixed 28d window. Bots deploying as a shared account = Non-Copilot. Correlation only.

panels.title: 📚 Learning Guide — How This Dashboard Works

options.content:
## 📖 Learning Guide: Deployment Frequency

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Counts distinct successful deployments divided by the number of weeks in the selected time window.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / GREATEST(1, EXTRACT(DAY FROM ($__timeTo()::timestamptz - $__timeFrom()::timestamptz)) / 7.0) AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
AND ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **GitHub-native only** — deployments via ArgoCD, Jenkins, or other tools not integrated with the GitHub Deployments API are invisible to this metric.
- **Environment field is free-text** — 'production', 'prod', and 'PROD' count as separate environments unless normalized.
- **Success state is caller-set** — the deployer marks a deployment 'success'; GitHub does not independently verify it.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether deployed code is actually healthy in production.
- Deployments that bypassed the GitHub Deployments API entirely.

## 📖 Learning Guide: Deployments per Week

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Time series bucketed by week using date_trunc('week', created_at). Counts distinct successful deployment IDs per week.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **date_trunc('week') starts on Monday** — a deployment on Sunday falls into the previous week bucket, which can misalign with sprint reporting.
- **created_at, not finished_at** — timestamps reflect deployment creation, not completion or marking as successful.
- **Zero-deployment weeks are gaps** — Grafana shows a line gap for weeks with no deployments (no zero-fill in the SQL result set).

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Deployment duration or rollback rates.
- Whether deploys targeted production or lower environments.

## 📖 Learning Guide: Deployments by Environment

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Bar chart grouped by the environment field from the deployments table, filtered to successful deployments in the time window.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  d.environment AS "Environment",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Free-text environment names** — 'production', 'prod', 'PROD', and 'Production' are four separate environments with no canonical mapping.
- **No environment hierarchy** — staging and production are treated equally; there is no parent-child relationship.
- **Only GitHub-native deploys** — external CD tools without GitHub Deployment API integration are absent.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Which environments are production-equivalent.
- Traffic or criticality weighting of each environment.

## 📖 Learning Guide: Deployment Success Rate

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Ratio of deployments with at least one 'success' status to total deployments. Uses NULLIF to prevent division by zero.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ds.state = 'success' THEN d.deployment_id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0), 1
) AS "Success Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **State is caller-set** — the deployer marks the deployment state; it is not independently verified by GitHub.
- **Deployments with no status rows** — a deployment created but never given a status ('pending') is in the denominator but never the numerator, deflating success rate.
- **Multiple status rows per deployment** — the query counts distinct deployment IDs with state='success', not individual status rows.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Post-deployment health or application availability.
- Partial failures (deploy succeeded but a feature flag is off, etc.).

## 📖 Learning Guide: Failed Deployments Timeline

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Weekly count of deployments where any status row has state='failure'.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Failed Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **'failure' vs 'error'** — the API distinguishes 'failure' (deploy ran but failed) from 'error' (deploy couldn't start). This query only captures 'failure'.
- **Retry artifacts** — a failed-then-retried deployment appears in both failure and success counts.
- **No root cause** — the API captures the state transition only; failure reason must be retrieved from CI logs separately.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Why deployments failed.
- Which service or component caused the failure.

## 📖 Learning Guide: Recent Deployments

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Table showing raw deployment records with their latest status. Uses LEFT JOIN so deployments with no status are still shown.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  d.sha,
  d.environment,
  ds.state AS status,
  d.creator_login,
  d.created_at
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **LEFT JOIN shows only latest status** — if a deployment has multiple status transitions, only the most-recent row is visible in the join.
- **SHA is the raw 40-char commit SHA** — no branch name or PR link is included in this view; bridge resolver data is not joined here.
- **creator_login is the API caller** — this is whoever triggered the deployment via the API (often a bot), not necessarily the developer whose code is deployed.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Which PR or branch was deployed.
- Whether a deployment was a rollback or a new feature.

panels.title: 📖 Deployment Frequency: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

options.content:
## 📖 Learning Guide: Deployment Frequency: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Splits weekly deployments by whether the deployment creator_login matches an active Copilot seat. Built with a WITH copilot_users CTE.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Attribution by deployer, not developer** — cohort split uses creator_login (who called the Deployments API), not the PR author. Bots or shared accounts inflate 'Non-Copilot'.
- **Fixed 28-day seat window** — the CTE uses NOW()-28d regardless of your Grafana time range. Historical comparisons always use the current seat roster.
- **Correlation only** — teams using Copilot may differ in many ways from those who don't.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether Copilot was used in any specific deployment.
- Causality between Copilot adoption and deployment frequency.

list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.label: Environment
list.name: environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

description: DORA Pillar: Deployment Frequency — Measures how often code is successfully deployed. Elite teams deploy multiple times per day.
